ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). The ONLY Haskell you may read is the SANCTIONED BSD library /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6/. Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md, and do NOT read any AUDIT.md anywhere in the cleanroom. No web access. You MAY read .spthy examples under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples/. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in workspace QUERIES.log (Session 12); record behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with probe provenance. Preserve OOM guards; live HS servers only on ports 3200-3299; STOP every server you start. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary.

Workspace: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/ (read/write freely, except AUDIT.md). Crate: workspace/graph-clean. Re-read BEHAVIOR.md (§3f + round-11 report), QUERIES.log Session 11, INTERFACE.md, and workspace/r11/ first — round 12 continues from round 11 (census: all cells 98.794%, wrapping 95.60%).

CONTEXT — corpus-scale measurement relayed from the open side (behavioral, with witnesses): against the 12,022-payload corpus your current model leaves 11,373 diverging cells in three families:
FAMILY A (DOMINANT, 8,564 cells — 75%): both sides wrap, but the break lands one element apart. Witness: !Handover_Session( KD2, <~AMF_ID, ~MME_ID, ~gNB_ID, ~eNB_ID, ~SUPI> ) first diverges at byte 159 — the reference breaks after ~gNB_ID, where your fill breaks one element earlier. This is the fill-width/rounding law at the margin: your allocation is close but off by roughly one element's width in a systematic subset. Mine the corpus for this family with your census tools (cluster by: receiver shape, sibling count/flats, tuple-arg position, how far the break is off), then design targeted live batteries around the cluster centroids — the goal is the exact fill width in the shapes where yours is off by one, and whether the discrepancy is in the numerator (shape surcharges), the denominator (sibling weighting), the rounding boundary, or a second-order term you have not modeled (e.g. interaction between the bonus and the fill numerator, or a different fill base for cells that triggered via the relief pass).
FAMILY B (1,659 cells): trigger false-negatives — your model keeps the cell flat where the reference wraps (often a )-peel). Witness: SndS( ~cid_S1, ~MME_ID, ~eNB_ID, <'ho_cmd', EN1> ) diverges at byte 50 — the reference peels the closing paren, you stay flat. Probe the trigger boundary in these shapes (last-arg tuple present — check the bonus gate's exact condition and whether some shapes get NO bonus where you grant one, or a lower budget than you compute).
FAMILY C (1,150 cells): trigger false-positives — you wrap where the reference stays flat. Witness: Resp_1( $I, $R, ~ltkR, ~ekR, 'g'^~ekI ) diverges at byte 37. Your round-11 relief pass cut ~330 of these; probe what distinguishes the remaining shapes (exponent/AC terms in the last arg? relief cascading more than one pass? charge basis for peel-only siblings?).
ACCEPTANCE: all tests green; roundtrip + alloc stay 12022/12022; fill_census before/after (all cells, wrapping, single-cell, multi-cell, false-flat, false-wrap); probe-battery byte-exactness; absent-override behavior regression-gated. Honest residue characterization if full closure is not reached — per-family counts with what was pinned vs still unexplained. Do NOT edit anything outside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	# graph-clean — adapter-facing width-input interface (round 11)
2	
3	Audience: the OPEN-side integrator wiring an adapter later. Everything here is
4	implemented and test-gated in `workspace/graph-clean`; semantics trace to
5	BEHAVIOR.md §3f (Sessions 10–11).
6	
7	## Round-11 status: internal widths are NOT how the reference decides
8	
9	Round-11 live probes (battery J, QUERIES.log Session 11) REFUTED the round-9/10
10	belief that the reference wraps cells on their UN-abbreviated internal widths:
11	abbreviated cells with internal widths 96–150 render FLAT when their display
12	text fits, and a sibling's budget follows the abbreviated display occupancy.
13	The reference lays out the POST-abbreviation display text everywhere probed.
14	Consequently an adapter should normally pass **no overrides at all** — the
15	display-text estimates are the probed reference behavior. The override surface
16	remains for callers that know better in exceptional cases, and every field
17	falls back per-field to the estimate, byte-identically.
18	
19	## Surface
20	
21	```rust
22	pub struct generate::CellWidths {
23	    pub occupancy:     Option<i64>, // row occupancy C (columns this cell counts
24	                                    // for in siblings' budgets + fill shares)
25	    pub bonus:         Option<i64>, // the cell's own pass-1 trigger-budget bonus
26	    pub fill_width:    Option<i64>, // fill numerator once the cell wraps
27	    pub trigger_width: Option<i64>, // effective self width in BOTH trigger
28	                                    // passes (incl. lone cells vs 87)
29	}
30	pub fn generate::group_widths_with(cells: &[String],
31	                                   overrides: &[Option<CellWidths>]) -> Vec<usize>;
32	// RawRule builders (one Option<CellWidths> per premise / conclusion cell):
33	RawRule::premise_widths(Vec<Option<CellWidths>>)
34	RawRule::conclusion_widths(Vec<Option<CellWidths>>)
35	```
36	
37	## Semantics (precise, round-11 laws)
38	
39	Per record group (premises, or conclusions; the info cell is its own group):
40	
41	- **occupancy**: `C_i = flat_i + rec_sur_i` where `rec_sur` is the recursive
42	  tuple/union surcharge — each tuple/union node contributes `elems + 1`,
43	  except nodes directly inside another tuple/union which contribute
44	  `elems − 1`;
45	- **trigger pass 1**: cell *i* wraps iff
46	  `eff_i > max(87 + bonus_i − Σ_{j≠i} C_j, 20)` (lone cell: budget exactly
47	  87), `eff_i` = display flat (or `trigger_width`), `bonus_i` = the largest
48	  `⌊elems/2⌋ + 2` over top-level tuple/union args (4 at ≥ 9 elems) applied
49	  ONLY when the fact's LAST argument is such a tuple/union, else 0;
50	- **fill** (only if wrapping): ribbon
51	  `clamp(hd(87·N_i / (N_i + Σ_{j≠i} w_j·C_j)), 20, flat_i − 1)` rounded
52	  half-DOWN, `N_i = flat_i + rec_sur7_i + nfunc_i` (per-node surcharges
53	  capped at 7), `w_j = 5/6` for a single-quoted-atom sibling when cell *i*
54	  has a top-level tuple/union arg, else 1; the cell is laid out at
55	  lineLength `⌊3·ribbon/2⌋`, ribbonsPerLine 1.5 (ragged HughesPJ fill; the
56	  tuple opener `<` may hang);
57	- **trigger pass 2 (relief)**: a pass-1-wrapping cell renders FLAT iff
58	  `eff_i ≤ max(87 − Σ_{j≠i} charge_j, 20)` where a truly-broken wrapping
59	  sibling (fill < flat − 2) charges its fill allocation and everything else
60	  charges `C_j`; no bonus here.
61	
62	Overrides substitute per FIELD (`C_i` ← `occupancy`, `bonus_i` ← `bonus`,
63	`N_i` ← `fill_width`, `eff_i` ← `trigger_width`); each absent field (or absent
64	entry, or a short/empty override slice) falls back to the display-text
65	estimate for that field. With every input absent the output is byte-identical
66	to `group_widths` — regression-gated by
67	`tests/generate_tests.rs::supplied_cell_widths_override_estimates`,
68	`::supplied_trigger_width_overrides_self_width`,
69	`::raw_rule_supplied_widths_reach_cells`, plus the corpus census.
70

192	### 3f. Record-cell term rendering + line wrapping (§ Round-3 item 1)
193	**Group structure** (byte-verified over 160 409 records): the label is
194	`{grp}|{grp}|{grp}` where the groups are the *non-empty* ones among
195	`[premises, info, conclusions]`, in that order; the **info** group is always
196	present (100 %). An empty premise or conclusion group is dropped (a source rule
197	renders `{info}|{concl}`).
198	- **Info cell**: `#<temporal> : <RuleName>` and, if the rule has action facts,
199	  `[<action>, …]` (e.g. `#i : I_Complete[Complete( … ), …]`, or bare `#vf.5 : Fresh`).
200	- **Premise/conclusion cell**: a single fact.
201	- **Fact spacing**: a fact pads — `Name( a, b )`, and `Name( )` with no args;
202	  a **function application** does not — `f(a, b)`; a **tuple** is `<a, b>`. Verified
203	  against the corpus.
204	- **Escaping**: inside a record label, `< > { } |` each get a leading backslash;
205	  everything else (quotes, `~ $ ^ * ⊕`, spaces) is literal.
206	- **Line wrapping FORMAT** (byte-exact). When a group breaks, physical lines are
207	  separated by `\l` (with a trailing `\l`), and each continuation line is indented
208	  with a ` ` run **equal to the column of the broken group's first element**
209	  (just after `( ` for a fact, after `<` for a tuple, after `[` for an action
210	  list). Verified across 188 192 wrapped cells.
211	- **Line wrapping DECISION — RESOLVED (Session 4): a fixed-width paragraph fill,
212	  width = 87 columns, measured per fact from column 0.** The Round-3 claim ("first
213	  line 5..87, not a flat width, not derivable") is **refuted**: it *is* a flat
214	  width. Established by driving crafted single-node theories
215	  (`Out(<'a01', …, 'aN'>)`, `Out(<'aa…a', 'y'>)`) through the live server and
216	  sweeping the term width one column at a time:
217	  - **W = 87 absolute, functor-invariant.** A fact stays on one line iff its flat
218	    rendering is ≤ 87 columns; at 88 it breaks. Tested with functor names of length
219	    2, 3, 6, 10 (`Ba`,`Out`,`Fact12`,`Factabcdef`): the boundary was **always** at
220	    flat width 87 fits / 88 breaks, so the budget is counted from the functor's own
221	    column 0, *including* the functor name (a longer name simply fits fewer args).
222	    Corpus-consistent: 99.2 % of 190 044 physical lines have fact-content ≤ 87; the
223	    residual are single unbreakable atoms wider than 87 (which overflow verbatim).
224	  - **Greedy paragraph fill.** When a group overflows, elements pack left-to-right;
225	    the separator `, ` trails the element it follows and stays on the line, and the
226	    next element wraps when it would pass column 87. Continuation lines use the
227	    §3f indent. Example `Out(<'a01'…'a12'>)` (flat 91): line 0 =
228	    `Out( <'a01', … 'a11', ` (eleven elements + trailing `, `, ending col 83),
229	    `'a12'>` on the next line, then the fact's `)` on its own line.
230	  - **Delimiter peel — BYTE-IMPLEMENTED (Session 5).** Re-probed the boundary with
231	    fresh single-node sweeps (`E10..E14`, `W69..W74`, ports 3210): a **tuple's `>`**
232	    stays with the last element iff it fits (`Out( <'aaa(72)', 'y'>` │ `)`), else it
233	    peels onto its own line at the tuple's **`<` column** (`W74`: `…'y'` │ `<5sp>>`
234	    │ `)`); a **fact's `)` ALWAYS** peels onto its own line at **col 0** once the
235	    fact wraps — even when it would fit (`E12`: `'a12'>` │ `)`) — because the padded
236	    ` )` space is the break. An **info action-list `]`** stays attached to its last
237	    action. An unbreakable atom wider than the budget overflows verbatim; only the
238	    trailing delimiters wrap.
239	  - **Continuation lookahead — RESOLVED (subsumed).** The old "first line 11,
240	    continuation 12" claim is a mis-read: the continuation packs **greedily to the
241	    same width 87** as the first line (`E13`→2, `E14`→3 elements on the second line,
242	    each ending well under 87). There is no ±1 combinator lookahead to reproduce.
243	  - **Info action-list = vertical `sep`, not fill.** An overflowing info cell puts
244	    **one action per line** (corpus `6738eb64…`: 4 actions → 4 lines, short actions
245	    included), `]` on the last; whereas a tuple / fact-arg list uses the greedy
246	    fill. Two distinct combinators, both byte-implemented.
247	  - **`graph-clean::render::wrap_cell`** implements all of the above (`layout_fact`
248	    fill+`)`-peel, `layout_tuple` fill+`>`-peel, `layout_info` vertical `sep`,
249	    `run_layout` greedy fill, `split_top_commas`), wired into
250	    `generate::build_record`; byte-verified against the 7 captured probe fixtures
251	    (`wrap_E11..E14`, `wrap_W71/W72/W74`).
252	  - **The wrap TRIGGER is a per-GROUP shared budget — RESOLVED (Session 6).** The
253	    old per-cell `flat > 87` decision is wrong for multi-cell groups. Each record
254	    group (`premises`, `info`, `conclusions`) is laid out **independently** (a
255	    conclusion cell's wrap ignores premise/info widths, and vice versa — live
256	    probes PM/R: a lone conclusion `Out` of flat 87 stays flat with premises of
257	    flat 69/103/198 and rule names of length 40). Within a group, the cells share
258	    a budget:
259	    > For a premise/conclusion group, let `T = Σ(flat width of every cell in the
260	    > group)`. Cell *i* wraps **iff** `flat_i > budget_i`, where
261	    > `budget_i = max(87 − (T − flat_i), 20)`. Equivalently: **cell *i* wraps iff
262	    > `T > 87` AND `flat_i > 20`.**
263	    Established by controlled live 2-/3-cell sweeps (QUERIES §6): with a fixed
264	    second cell of flat *q*, the first cell's fit/wrap boundary is exactly
265	    `flat = 87 − q` (q=11 → 76/77, q=28 → 58/61, q=48 → 39/40, q=68 → 19/22); a
266	    third cell adds into the same Σ (3-cell [p,28,28] flips at `flat = 87−56 = 31`).
267	    The **floor 20** is a per-cell minimum budget (a cell of flat ≤ 20 never wraps;
268	    live: Fb flat 98 forces the sibling budget negative yet the sibling fits at
269	    flat ≤ 20, wraps at 21). Verified by hand on `ref_raw_1_1` (Wide rule): premise
270	    `In` flat 67 does **not** wrap (budget 87−10 = 77) while conclusion `Big` flat
271	    68 **does** (budget 87−(25+14) = 48); `Ack` flat 25 wraps (budget 20), `Out(h)`
272	    flat 14 fits — all four correct.
273	  - **The `#t : Rule[…]` INFO cell** is its own single-cell group (budget 87,
274	    independent of prem/concl), with an added rule: **an info cell with ≥ 2 action
275	    facts ALWAYS goes vertical** (one action per line), regardless of width — a
276	    ≤ 1-action info wraps only when its flat > 87. Corpus census: 0 non-wrapped
277	    info cells carry a top-level action-list comma (all ≥2-action infos wrap).
278	  - **Corpus validation at scale.** The rule matches actual `\l` on **99.635 %** of
279	    776 259 cells and **98.324 %** of 160 409 records (info cells 99.968 %). Every
280	    one of the 2 831 residual cells is within ~1–2 columns of its budget (mismatch
281	    `T`-distribution clusters entirely at 79 ≤ T ≤ 95). At that exact boundary the
282	    outcome is a **±1 HughesPJ `fits`/ribbon rounding artifact** (live: at a group
283	    total of exactly `budget+1` the fit/wrap flips with atom parity; at `budget+5`
284	    it wraps cleanly), depending on the cell's token structure, order, and the port
285	    marker widths — reproducing it byte-exactly needs the GPL pretty-printer's
286	    internal `fits`. Two residuals remain **[GAP]**: (a) this ±1 boundary flip, and
287	    (b) the exact greedy fill width when a *multi-cell* cell wraps (live probeB: the
288	    per-line element count is not a clean function of the budget). The budget
289	    **trigger** itself (does a cell wrap) is byte-exact away from the boundary.
290	  - **The FILL width once a cell wraps ≠ the trigger budget — REFINED (Session 7).**
291	    The wrap *trigger* is the flat-sum budget above (which cell breaks). The *fill*
292	    width (how a broken cell packs) is **wider**: a sibling that itself wraps
293	    occupies only the width it is ALLOCATED, not its flat width, so the remaining
294	    cell packs more elements per line. Live datum (`Wide` conclusion group
295	    `[Ack 25, Big 68, Out 11]`): the 68-flat `Big` cell packs **8** tuple elements
296	    on line 0, as if its budget were **56** — not the flat-sum `87−(25+11) = 51`
297	    (which packs 7). The fill budgets come from a **smallest-flat-first** greedy
298	    allocation: process cells by increasing flat; each cell's budget is
299	    `max(87 − Σ others' allocations, 20)` where a processed sibling contributes
300	    `min(flat, its budget)` and an un-processed (wider) one its full flat. For
301	    `Wide`: `Out`(11) fits; `Ack`, seen while `Big` is still at flat 68, is squeezed
302	    to budget 20 and wraps (breaking after `~n.4`); `Big`, placed last, sees `Ack`'s
303	    allocation 20 and gets `87 − 20 − 11 = 56` → 8 elements. This reproduces the
304	    whole `Wide` record byte-exact (fixture `wide_record.dot`, verified against the
305	    live `wide_group.dot` capture) and, applied to every wrapping prem/concl cell in
306	    the corpus, raises multi-line fill byte-exactness from **41.45 %** (flat-sum) to
307	    **44.11 %** (best of the models tried: flat-sum 41.45 %, sibling-occ 43.29 %,
308	    this greedy `min(flat,budget)` 44.11 %). The fill is nonetheless still largely
309	    the GPL `fillSep`'s `fits`, not a per-cell budget — 56 % of multi-line cells
310	    remain **[GAP]**: a single non-wrapping sibling of flat 10 barely reduces a wide
311	    cell's fill budget (live batchA: still 11 elements), whereas a wrapping sibling
312	    of the same flat reduces it a lot, so the sibling contribution is not a clean
313	    function of its width (batchA fitting-sibling vs Wide wrapping-sibling).
314	  - **The TRIGGER residual, decomposed — Session 7.** The 2 831 flat-sum
315	    mispredictions split, for prem/concl cells (2 779), into two mechanisms, each
316	    beyond a rendering-crate closed form:
317	    * **false-positive (1 320; group total 88–97):** flat-sum predicts wrap but the
318	      cell FITS, because a *wide* sibling wraps and frees room for a *small* cell
319	      (live hg68: `[Ta, Hg 68]` → `Ta` fits at flat 21 because `Hg` wraps and
320	      occupies 66, so `Ta`'s budget is `87 − 66 = 21`, not `87 − 68`). This
321	      occ-relief is the greedy printer's coupled `fits`: it saves a small cell but
322	      NOT a large one beside a comparable sibling (live `[49,58]` both wrap), and no
323	      closed rule captures it — feeding the ACTUAL occupied widths back universally
324	      is *worse* (0.82 % vs 0.45 %), so flat-sum stands.
325	    * **false-negative (1 459; group total 79–87):** flat-sum predicts fit but the
326	      cell WRAPS. **954 (65 %)** are explained by the wrap being decided on the
327	      **unabbreviated** term: e.g. `St_3_eNB( ~eNB_ID, KD19, ~MME_ID, EN2, ~gNB_ID )`
328	      displays at flat 48 but its abbreviations (`KD19`, `EN2`) expand well past 87,
329	      so it breaks; abbreviations are substituted into the already-broken layout.
330	      This is structurally outside a rendering crate that receives POST-abbreviation
331	      cell text — an honest **[GAP]**. The remaining ~505 are the ±1 `fits` boundary.
332	  - **Order-independence — re-confirmed (Session 7).** Cell ORDER within a group
333	    never affects a wrap boundary, even for comparable cells at the margin and even
334	    with wrapping siblings (live G: `[A,B]` ≡ `[B,A]`, and `[A,30,30]` gives the
335	    same `A` boundary in every position). So the fill allocation is order-free
336	    (sorted by flat, not by position).
337	  - **REVISED (Session 9) — the per-group share is a TWO-LAYER trigger + fill
338	    allocation with SHAPE-CORRECTED occupancies; the round-8 proportional rule is
339	    superseded (see the Session-9 items below and the Round-9 report).**
340	  - **RESOLVED (Session 8) — the fill is HughesPJ `fill` with a 1.5 RIBBON, and the
341	    per-group share is PROPORTIONAL.** With the sanctioned BSD `pretty` library
342	    (Text.PrettyPrint.HughesPJ) ported faithfully, two parameters close most of the
343	    round-7 fill GAP:
344	    * **Ribbon.** A record cell is laid out with **ribbonsPerLine = 1.5** (the
345	      HughesPJ default): the fit boundary is the *ribbon* (= 87 for a lone cell),
346	      and the line length is `1.5 ×` the ribbon. Because lineLength > ribbon,
347	      HughesPJ `fill` produces **ragged** paragraph fills — a physical line can be
348	      SHORTER than a later one. Corpus proof: `St_1_gNB( ~gNB_ID, KD8, KD1, '0',
349	      AM2, GN1 )` wraps as `~gNB_ID, KD8,` (2 args) │ `KD1, '0', AM2, GN1` (4 wider
350	      args, aligned under `(`) │ `)`. No greedy fill (lineLength == ribbon) can ever
351	      produce a shorter line0; the round-7 model always could not, which is why 56 %
352	      of multi-line cells were a GAP. Re-probed live (`layout_at` LINELEN/RIBBON):
353	      ribbon 24–26 (lineLength 36–39) reproduces `St_1_gNB` byte-exact; all prior
354	      fixtures (E11–E14, W71/W72/W74, Ack, Big, In) still reproduce under rpl=1.5.
355	    * **Per-group share = proportional.** Cell *i* of a prem/concl group gets fit
356	      budget (ribbon) `B_i = max(round(87 · flat_i / T), 20)`, `T = Σ flat_j`. For a
357	      lone cell `T = flat` ⇒ `B = 87` (recovers the boundary). `T ≤ 87` ⇒ every
358	      `B_i ≥ flat_i` ⇒ nothing wraps. Wide conclusions `[Ack 25, Big 68, Out 11]`,
359	      `T = 104`: `Ack 21` (wraps), `Big 57` (8 tuple elems on line 0), `Out 9→20`
360	      (fits) — byte-exact. The 20 floor is the §3f per-cell minimum.
361	    * **The engine at the right budget IS byte-exact** (the cell doc — fact `fsep` /
362	      tuple `fcat` / info `vcat` — is correct): single-cell wrapping cells match
363	      **94.7 %** (allocator-independent; residual = abbreviation-expansion + ±1).
364	    * **Corpus census (12 022 dot).** Wrapping-cell byte-exactness rose from ~44 %
365	      (round-7 greedy) to **81.1 %** overall (single-cell 94.7 %, multi-cell 80.0 %).
366	      Proportional is the best of the allocators tried (smallest-first 62.6 %,
367	      flat-sum 59.3 %, prop-ceil 79.9 %, reserve-small 77.4 %).
368	    * **Residual [GAP], honestly.** (a) ±1 `fits` boundary — proportional lands the
369	      budget within a few columns of the reference's own coupled `fits`; at a bucket
370	      edge (e.g. `In_S( 'D2', 'H2', spkDD )` where prop=24.2→24 but the reference

379	  - **RESOLVED (Session 9, SUPERSEDED by Session 10) — the group WRAP TRIGGER
380	    is size-corrected flat-sum with the `elems + 1` law.** Six round-10 live
381	    batteries (audit RA–RE; func FB–FE; quote QA–QC; tuple/union pins PA–PF /
382	    UEV / TN / TB / UN / UB — QUERIES.log Session 10) on top of the round-8/9
383	    grids pin the wrap decision to:
384	    > Cell *j* occupies `C_j = flat_j + Σ_{top-level tuple/union args}
385	    > (elems + 1)`. Cell *i*'s trigger budget in a multi-cell row is
386	    > `max(87 + bonus_i − Σ_{j≠i} C_j, 20)` with `bonus_i` = the largest
387	    > `⌊elems/2⌋ + 2` over its own tuple/union args (an arg with ≥ 9 elements
388	    > contributes 4; no such arg ⇒ 0); it wraps iff `flat_i` exceeds the
389	    > budget. A lone cell's budget is exactly 87.
390	    The `elems + 1` occupancy is boundary-exact at n = 2, 3, 4, 6 (tuples)
391	    and 3, 5, 8 (unions): the 45-argfact partner flips exactly one column
392	    after `C` crosses 42 in every sweep, refuting round-9's `2n − 4` (which

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Round-11 miss inventory over bands6.tsv (14-field cells).
3	
4	Replicates the shipped round-10 model (generate::group_widths) exactly and
5	classifies every cell: OK / FF (false flat: obs wrap, pred fit) / FW (false
6	wrap: obs flat, pred wrap) / FM (both wrap, predicted L outside the observed
7	band).  Aggregates by group abbreviation content and shape for family
8	clustering (open-side round-10 integration families 1-4).
9	"""
10	import sys, math, collections
11	
12	def parse(path):
13	    rows = []
14	    for line in open(path):
15	        p = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
16	        if len(p) != 6:
17	            continue
18	        cells = []
19	        ok = True
20	        for cc in p[5].split("|"):
21	            seg = cc.split(":")
22	            if len(seg) != 14:
23	                ok = False
24	                break
25	            f = int(seg[0]); st = seg[1]
26	            (dtop, drec, nq, sqa, nargs, nfunc, nabbr, ctup, bmax,
27	             tup, uni, cbmax) = map(int, seg[2:])
28	            cells.append(dict(f=f, st=st, wrap=not st.startswith("F"),
29	                              sqa=sqa, nfunc=nfunc, nabbr=nabbr,
30	                              tup=tup, uni=uni, bonus=cbmax))
31	        if ok and cells:
32	            rows.append((p[0], p[1], cells))
33	    return rows
34	
35	def band_runs(st):
36	    """Observed-valid L set for a wrapping cell: list of (lo,hi); [] if NONE."""
37	    if st == "NONE":
38	        return []
39	    return [tuple(map(int, r.split("-"))) for r in st.split(",")]
40	
41	def in_band(st, L):
42	    if st.startswith("F"):
43	        return st != "FNONE"
44	    return any(a <= L <= b for a, b in band_runs(st))
45	
46	def shipped_widths(cells):
47	    """Exact mirror of generate::group_widths_with(cells, &[])."""
48	    n = len(cells)
49	    C = [c["f"] + c["tup"] + c["uni"] for c in cells]
50	    S = sum(C)
51	    out = []
52	    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
53	        f = c["f"]
54	        if n == 1:
55	            # a lone cell is laid out at 87 and wraps iff its flat exceeds it
56	            out.append((87, f > 87))
57	            continue
58	        b_t = max(87 + c["bonus"] - (S - C[i]), 20)
59	        if f <= b_t:
60	            out.append((max(b_t, f), False))
61	            continue
62	        num = float(f + c["uni"] + c["nfunc"])
63	        t = num
64	        for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
65	            if j != i:
66	                w = 5.0/6.0 if (cj["sqa"] and c["tup"] > 0) else 1.0
67	                t += w * C[j]
68	        b = math.floor(87.0 * num / t + 0.5)
69	        b = max(20, min(b, max(f - 1, 20)))
70	        out.append((b, True))
71	    return out
72	
73	def main():
74	    rows = parse(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "bands6.tsv")
75	    cnt = collections.Counter()
76	    # family clustering pools
77	    fm_cluster = collections.Counter()   # (grp nabbr>0, n, nwrap_obs) for FM
78	    fw_cluster = collections.Counter()   # FW context
79	    ff_cluster = collections.Counter()
80	    fm_delta = collections.Counter()     # predicted-L minus nearest band edge (abbr-free)
81	    fm_examples = []
82	    fw_examples = []
83	    ff_examples = []
84	    for fname, kind, cells in rows:
85	        n = len(cells)
86	        widths = shipped_widths(cells)
87	        grp_abbr = sum(c["nabbr"] for c in cells)
88	        nwrap_obs = sum(1 for c in cells if c["wrap"])
89	        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
90	            b, pred_wrap = widths[i]
91	            L = 3 * b // 2
92	            if c["wrap"] and not pred_wrap:
93	                cnt["FF"] += 1
94	                ff_cluster[(grp_abbr > 0, n, c["nabbr"] > 0)] += 1
95	                if len(ff_examples) < 8:
96	                    ff_examples.append((fname, kind, n, i, c["f"], c["st"], b))
97	            elif not c["wrap"] and pred_wrap:
98	                cnt["FW"] += 1
99	                # does some OTHER cell in the row wrap (family-3 shape)?
100	                sib_wraps = any(cj["wrap"] for j, cj in enumerate(cells) if j != i)
101	                fw_cluster[(grp_abbr > 0, n, sib_wraps)] += 1
102	                if len(fw_examples) < 8:
103	                    fw_examples.append((fname, kind, n, i, c["f"], b,
104	                                        [(cj["f"], cj["st"][:12]) for cj in cells]))
105	            elif c["wrap"] and pred_wrap:
106	                if in_band(c["st"], L):
107	                    cnt["OK_fill"] += 1
108	                else:
109	                    cnt["FM"] += 1
110	                    fm_cluster[(grp_abbr > 0, n, nwrap_obs)] += 1
111	                    if grp_abbr == 0:
112	                        runs = band_runs(c["st"])
113	                        if runs:
114	                            # signed distance to nearest run edge
115	                            best = None
116	                            for a, bb in runs:
117	                                d = 0 if a <= L <= bb else (L - bb if L > bb else L - a)
118	                                if best is None or abs(d) < abs(best):
119	                                    best = d
120	                            fm_delta[max(-15, min(15, best))] += 1
121	                        else:
122	                            fm_delta["NONE"] += 1
123	                        if len(fm_examples) < 12 and n >= 2:
124	                            fm_examples.append((fname, kind, n, i, c["f"], c["st"], b, L,
125	                                                [(cj["f"], cj["tup"], cj["uni"], cj["wrap"]) for cj in cells]))
126	            else:
127	                cnt["OK_flat"] += 1

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-11 batteries J/K.

J (probeJ.spthy): abbreviation internal-layout (families 2+4).  Each rule
  builds a state fact carrying an abbreviable term T = h('a...a') (len >= 10,
  occurs >= 2 in the system via producer conclusion + consumer premise), so the
  graph abbreviates T to H1 and the record cells show the abbreviated display
  text.  Captured TWICE: default and ?unabbreviate= (internal ground truth).
K (probeK.spthy): shape pins -- nested-tuple occupancy (trigger), pair/6-tuple
  receiver fill numerators vs sibling sweep, opener hangs for tuple-in-func /
  union / wide-first-element tuples, and exact pair-of-tuples witness replicas."""

def pvars(n, off=0):
    a = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
    return ["$" + a[(i + off) // 26] + a[(i + off) % 26] for i in range(n)]

def longvar(ln, tag):
    body = ("q" + tag).ljust(ln - 1, "a")
    assert len(body) + 1 == ln
    return "$" + body

def argfact(name, flat, tag, off):
    minL = 2 + len(tag)
    k = (flat - len(name) - 4 - minL) // 5
    if k < 0:
        k = 0
    L1 = flat - len(name) - 4 - 5 * k
    assert L1 >= minL, (name, flat, k, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(k, off)
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

class B:
    def __init__(s, thy, builtins=None):
        s.thy, s.builtins = thy, builtins
        s.rules, s.lemmas, s.names, s.n = [], [], [], 0
    def emit_raw(s, name, body, lemma):
        s.rules.append(body)
        s.lemmas.append(lemma)
        s.names.append(name)
        s.n += 1
    def emit(s, name, prems, concls, comment):
        body = (f"// {comment}\nrule {name}:\n  [ {', '.join(prems)} ]\n"
                f"  --[ F{s.n}() ]->\n  [ {', '.join(concls)} ]\n")
        lemma = f"lemma l_{name}:\n  exists-trace \"Ex #i. F{s.n}() @ #i\"\n"
        s.emit_raw(name, body, lemma)
    def write(s, path):
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            f.write(f"theory {s.thy}\nbegin\n\n")
            if s.builtins:
                f.write(f"builtins: {s.builtins}\n")
            f.write("\n")
            for r in s.rules:
                f.write(r + "\n")
            for l in s.lemmas:
                f.write(l + "\n")
            f.write("end\n")
        with open(path.replace(".spthy", ".names"), "w") as f:
            f.write("\n".join(s.names) + "\n")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-12 miss inventory over bands9.tsv (20-field cells, round-11 dump).

Replicates the SHIPPED round-11 model (generate::group_widths_with, no
overrides) exactly, classifies every cell OK / FF / FW / FM, and clusters the
FM (family A) misses: delta from predicted L to the nearest observed band
edge, receiver shape, sibling profile, break direction, parity of the
predicted ribbon, and relief involvement.  Also scores L-mapping variants
(floor vs ceil lineLength) as a candidate second-order term.

Cell fields (band_dump round-11):
  0 flat  1 status  2 dtop 3 drec 4 nq 5 sqa 6 nargs 7 nfunc 8 nabbr
  9 ctup 10 bmax 11 tup_sur 12 uni_sur 13 cbmax 14 rec_sur 15 cnargs
  16 last_tup 17 rec5 18 rec7 19 rec9
"""
import sys, math, collections

def parse(path):
    rows = []
    for line in open(path):
        p = line.rstrip("\n").split("\t")
        if len(p) != 6:
            continue
        cells = []
        ok = True
        for cc in p[5].split("|"):
            seg = cc.split(":")
            if len(seg) != 20:
                ok = False
                break
            f = int(seg[0]); st = seg[1]
            (dtop, drec, nq, sqa, nargs, nfunc, nabbr, ctup, bmax,
             tup_sur, uni_sur, cbmax, rec_sur, cnargs, last_tup,
             rec5, rec7, rec9) = map(int, seg[2:])
            cells.append(dict(f=f, st=st, wrap=not st.startswith("F"),
                              sqa=sqa, nfunc=nfunc, nabbr=nabbr,
                              tup=tup_sur, uni=uni_sur, bonus=cbmax,
                              rec=rec_sur, rec7=rec7, last=last_tup,
                              nargs=cnargs, nq=nq))
        if ok and cells:
            rows.append((p[0], p[1], cells))
    return rows

def band_runs(st):
    if st == "NONE" or st.startswith("F"):
        return []
    return [tuple(map(int, r.split("-"))) for r in st.split(",")]

def hd(x):
    fl = math.floor(x)
    return int(fl if abs(x - fl - 0.5) < 1e-9 else math.floor(x + 0.5))

def shipped(cells):
    """Round-11 group_widths_with(cells, &[]) -> list of (kind, b, extras).
    kind: 'flat' (pred no wrap) | 'wrap' (pred wrap, b = fill ribbon).
    extras: dict with pass diagnostics."""
    n = len(cells)
    C = [c["f"] + c["rec"] for c in cells]
    Ctot = sum(C)
    out = []
    # pass 1
    budget1, wrap1 = [], []
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        bonus = c["bonus"] if c["last"] else 0
        b1 = 87 if n == 1 else max(87 + bonus - (Ctot - C[i]), 20)
        budget1.append(b1)
        wrap1.append(c["f"] > b1)
    # pass-1 fills
    fill1 = [None] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not wrap1[i]:
            continue
        if n == 1:
            fill1[i] = 87
            continue
        num = float(c["f"] + c["rec7"] + c["nfunc"])
        t = num
        for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
            if j != i:
                w = 5.0 / 6.0 if (cj["sqa"] and (c["tup"] + c["uni"]) > 0) else 1.0
                t += w * C[j]
        b = hd(87.0 * num / t)
        b = max(20, min(b, max(c["f"] - 1, 20)))
        fill1[i] = b
    # pass 2 relief
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not wrap1[i]:
            out.append(("flat", max(budget1[i], c["f"]), dict(pass1=False)))
            continue
        saved = False
        b2 = None
        if n > 1:
            tot = 0
            for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
                if j != i:
                    truly = wrap1[j] and fill1[j] is not None and fill1[j] < cj["f"] - 2
                    tot += fill1[j] if truly else C[j]
            b2 = max(87 - tot, 20)
            if c["f"] <= b2:
                saved = True
        if saved:
            out.append(("flat", max(b2, c["f"]), dict(pass1=True, saved=True)))
        else:
            out.append(("wrap", fill1[i], dict(pass1=True, saved=False, b2=b2,
                                               b1=budget1[i])))
    return out

def main():
    rows = parse(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "bands9.tsv")
    lmaps = {
        "floor(3b/2)": lambda b: 3 * b // 2,
        "ceil(3b/2)":  lambda b: (3 * b + 1) // 2,
    }
    cnt = {k: collections.Counter() for k in lmaps}
    # FM clustering under the SHIPPED floor map
    fm_delta = collections.Counter()      # signed L-delta to nearest band edge
    fm_bparity = collections.Counter()    # (b odd?, delta)
    fm_shape = collections.Counter()
    fm_relief = collections.Counter()
    fm_sibs = collections.Counter()
    fm_ex = collections.defaultdict(list)
    ff_shape = collections.Counter()
    ff_ex = []
    fw_shape = collections.Counter()
    fw_ex = []
    for fname, kind, cells in rows:
        n = len(cells)
        preds = shipped(cells)
        nwrap_obs = sum(1 for c in cells if c["wrap"])
        grp_abbr = sum(c["nabbr"] for c in cells)
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            pk, b, extras = preds[i]
            runs = band_runs(c["st"])
            for mname, lm in lmaps.items():
                L = lm(b)
                if c["wrap"] and pk == "flat":
                    cnt[mname]["FF"] += 1
                elif (not c["wrap"]) and pk == "wrap":
                    cnt[mname]["FW"] += 1
                elif c["wrap"]:
                    if c["st"] == "NONE":
                        cnt[mname]["NONE"] += 1
                    elif any(a <= L <= bb for a, bb in runs):
                        cnt[mname]["OK_fill"] += 1
                    else:
                        cnt[mname]["FM"] += 1
                else:
                    cnt[mname]["OK_flat"] += 1
            # detailed clustering under the shipped floor map
            L = 3 * b // 2
            if c["wrap"] and pk == "wrap" and c["st"] != "NONE" and runs \
               and not any(a <= L <= bb for a, bb in runs):
                best = None
                for a, bb in runs:
                    d = L - bb if L > bb else L - a
                    if best is None or abs(d) < abs(best):
                        best = d
                fm_delta[max(-25, min(25, best))] += 1
                fm_bparity[(b % 2, max(-4, min(4, best)))] += 1
                fm_shape[(c["tup"] > 0, c["uni"] > 0, c["nfunc"] > 0,
                          c["nabbr"] > 0, c["last"])] += 1
                fm_relief[(n, nwrap_obs, extras.get("saved"),
                           b == 20, best < 0)] += 1

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-12: diagnose the +1/-1 FM band-edge families.

For every wrapping predicted-wrap cell (shipped round-11 model), compute the
raw proportional quotient q = 87*N/t, its fractional part, the predicted
ribbon b = hd(q), L = 3b//2, and the position of L in the observed band.
Histogram frac for: OK cells, FM delta=+1, FM delta=-1, other FM.
Also: OK-cell margin to band edges (would a global -1 L shift break them?),
and re-score candidate variants:
  A  b = hd(q)          L = 3b//2          (shipped)
  B  b = floor(q)       L = 3b//2
  C  b = hd(q)          L = 3b//2 - 1
  D  b = hd(q) - 1      L = 3b//2
  E  b = hd(q)          L = (3b-1)//2      (even-b down 1)
  F  b = floor(q + 1/3) L = 3b//2
  G  q = 87*N/t with t = N + sum C_j + 1   hd, floor L
  H  q = (87*N - t/2)/t  (= 87N/t - 1/2)   hd == floor(87N/t) identical to B
  I  b = hd(86.5*N/t)   L = 3b//2
"""
import sys, math, collections
from eval12 import parse, band_runs, hd

def shipped_ctx(cells):
    n = len(cells)
    C = [c["f"] + c["rec"] for c in cells]
    Ctot = sum(C)
    budget1, wrap1 = [], []
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        bonus = c["bonus"] if c["last"] else 0
        b1 = 87 if n == 1 else max(87 + bonus - (Ctot - C[i]), 20)
        budget1.append(b1)
        wrap1.append(c["f"] > b1)
    qs = [None] * n
    fill1 = [None] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not wrap1[i]:
            continue
        if n == 1:
            fill1[i] = 87.0
            qs[i] = 87.0
            continue
        num = float(c["f"] + c["rec7"] + c["nfunc"])
        t = num
        for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
            if j != i:
                w = 5.0 / 6.0 if (cj["sqa"] and (c["tup"] + c["uni"]) > 0) else 1.0
                t += w * C[j]
        qs[i] = 87.0 * num / t
        fill1[i] = float(max(20, min(hd(qs[i]), max(c["f"] - 1, 20))))
    relief = [None] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not wrap1[i]:
            continue
        if n > 1:
            tot = 0.0
            for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
                if j != i:
                    truly = wrap1[j] and fill1[j] is not None and fill1[j] < cj["f"] - 2
                    tot += fill1[j] if truly else C[j]
            relief[i] = max(87.0 - tot, 20.0)
    return wrap1, qs, relief

def clamp_b(b, f):
    return max(20, min(b, max(f - 1, 20)))

def main():
    rows = parse(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "../r11/bands9.tsv")
    frac_hist = {k: collections.Counter() for k in ("OK", "FM+1", "FM-1", "FMother")}
    margin_lo = collections.Counter()
    margin_hi = collections.Counter()
    variants = {
        "A hd,floorL":    lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(hd(q), f) // 2,
        "B floor,floorL": lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(math.floor(q), f) // 2,
        "C hd,L-1":       lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(hd(q), f) // 2 - 1,
        "D hd-1,floorL":  lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(hd(q) - 1, f) // 2,
        "E hd,(3b-1)//2": lambda q, f: (3 * clamp_b(hd(q), f) - 1) // 2,
        "F floor(q+1/3)": lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(math.floor(q + 1.0 / 3.0), f) // 2,
        "I hd(q*86.5/87)":lambda q, f: 3 * clamp_b(hd(q * 86.5 / 87.0), f) // 2,
    }
    vcnt = {k: collections.Counter() for k in variants}
    for fname, kind, cells in rows:
        wrap1, qs, relief = shipped_ctx(cells)
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if not c["wrap"] or c["st"] == "NONE":
                continue
            if not wrap1[i]:
                continue  # FF handled elsewhere
            if relief[i] is not None and c["f"] <= relief[i]:
                continue  # relief-saved (pred flat): FF cell
            q = qs[i]
            runs = band_runs(c["st"])
            if not runs:
                continue
            b = clamp_b(hd(q), c["f"])
            L = 3 * b // 2
            inb = any(a <= L <= bb for a, bb in runs)
            clamped = (b != hd(q))
            if inb:
                key = "OK"
                best = 0
                lo_m = min(L - a for a, bb in runs if a <= L <= bb)
                hi_m = min(bb - L for a, bb in runs if a <= L <= bb)
                margin_lo[min(lo_m, 6)] += 1
                margin_hi[min(hi_m, 6)] += 1
            else:
                best = None
                for a, bb in runs:
                    d = L - bb if L > bb else L - a
                    if best is None or abs(d) < abs(best):
                        best = d
                key = "FM+1" if best == 1 else ("FM-1" if best == -1 else "FMother")
            fr = q - math.floor(q)
            bucket = ("CLAMP" if clamped else round(math.floor(fr * 10) / 10.0, 1))
            frac_hist[key][bucket] += 1
            for vn, vf in variants.items():
                Lv = vf(q, c["f"])
                vcnt[vn]["ok" if any(a <= Lv <= bb for a, bb in runs) else "miss"] += 1
    print("frac histogram (floor(10*frac)/10; CLAMP = clamped to floor/flat-1):")
    for key in ("OK", "FM+1", "FM-1", "FMother"):
        tot = sum(frac_hist[key].values())
        print(f"  {key} (n={tot}):")
        for bucket in ["CLAMP"] + [round(x / 10.0, 1) for x in range(10)]:
            v = frac_hist[key].get(bucket, 0)
            if v:
                print(f"    {bucket}: {v} ({100.0*v/max(tot,1):.1f}%)")
    print("\nOK margin to band lo (clamped 6):", dict(sorted(margin_lo.items())))
    print("OK margin to band hi (clamped 6):", dict(sorted(margin_hi.items())))
    print("\nvariant scores (in-band / miss over wrap-predicted banded cells):")
    for vn in variants:
        print(f"  {vn}: {dict(vcnt[vn])}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-12: needed-Δq analysis. For every banded wrap-predicted cell compute
the allowed ribbon interval B_ok = {b : 3b//2 in band} (union), q, and the
signed correction dq needed to land hd(q) in B_ok (0 if already OK).
Group mean/quantiles of dq by shape features to identify the biased term.
"""
import sys, math, collections
from eval12 import parse, band_runs, hd
from frac12 import shipped_ctx, clamp_b

def allowed_bs(runs, f):
    ok = set()
    for b in range(20, max(f, 90) + 2):
        L = 3 * b // 2
        if any(a <= L <= bb for a, bb in runs):
            ok.add(b)
    return ok

def main():
    rows = parse(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "../r11/bands9.tsv")
    # per-feature accumulation: feature -> [sum_dq, n, n_pos, n_neg]
    feats = collections.defaultdict(lambda: [0.0, 0, 0, 0])
    def acc(name, val, dq):
        s = feats[(name, val)]
        s[0] += dq; s[1] += 1
        if dq > 0: s[2] += 1
        if dq < 0: s[3] += 1
    for fname, kind, cells in rows:
        wrap1, qs, relief = shipped_ctx(cells)
        n = len(cells)
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if not c["wrap"] or c["st"] == "NONE" or not wrap1[i]:
                continue
            if relief[i] is not None and c["f"] <= relief[i]:
                continue
            runs = band_runs(c["st"])
            if not runs:
                continue
            q = qs[i]
            b = clamp_b(hd(q), c["f"])
            if b != hd(q):
                continue  # clamped cells are insensitive to q
            ok = allowed_bs(runs, c["f"])
            ok = {x for x in ok if 20 <= x <= max(c["f"] - 1, 20)}
            if not ok:
                continue
            if b in ok:
                dq = 0.0
            else:
                # distance from q to the nearest q' with hd(q') in ok
                cands = []
                for t in ok:
                    # q' range mapping to t under hd: [t-0.5, t+0.5] (with .5 down)
                    lo, hi = t - 0.5, t + 0.5
                    if q < lo:
                        cands.append(lo - q + 1e-6)
                    elif q > hi:
                        cands.append(-(q - hi) - 1e-6)
                    else:
                        cands.append(0.0)
                dq = min(cands, key=abs)
            sibs = [cj for j, cj in enumerate(cells) if j != i]
            sib_rec = sum(cj["rec"] for cj in sibs)
            sib_rec7 = sum(cj["rec7"] for cj in sibs)
            sib_wrapping = sum(1 for cj in sibs if cj["wrap"])
            sib_sqa = sum(1 for cj in sibs if cj["sqa"])
            acc("nfunc", min(c["nfunc"], 4), dq)
            acc("rec7", min(c["rec7"], 8), dq)
            acc("rec_minus_rec7", min(c["rec"] - c["rec7"], 6), dq)
            acc("tup", c["tup"] > 0, dq)
            acc("uni", c["uni"] > 0, dq)
            acc("nabbr", min(c["nabbr"], 4), dq)
            acc("last", c["last"], dq)
            acc("bonus_last", c["bonus"] if c["last"] else 0, dq)
            acc("n", min(n, 6), dq)
            acc("nwrapobs", (min(n, 4), min(sib_wrapping, 3)), dq)
            acc("sib_rec", min(sib_rec, 10), dq)
            acc("sib_rec_gap", min(sib_rec - sib_rec7, 6), dq)
            acc("sib_sqa", min(sib_sqa, 2), dq)
            acc("nq", min(c["nq"], 4), dq)
            acc("nargs", min(c["nargs"], 8), dq)
    for (name, val), (s, cnt, npos, nneg) in sorted(feats.items()):
        print(f"{name:14} {str(val):10} n={cnt:6} mean_dq={s/max(cnt,1):+.4f} pos={npos:5} ({100.0*npos/max(cnt,1):4.1f}%) neg={nneg:5} ({100.0*nneg/max(cnt,1):4.1f}%)")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import math, sys, collections

from eval12 import parse, band_runs, hd

corpus = parse("../r11/bands9.tsv")

def model(cells, blaw):
    n = len(cells)
    C = [c["f"] + c["rec"] for c in cells]
    S = sum(C)
    def bonus_of(c):
        if blaw == "last":
            return c["bonus"] if c["last"] else 0
        return c["bonus"] if (c["last"] and c["nargs"] == 1) else 0
    bon = [bonus_of(c) for c in cells]
    pw, fills = [], [None]*n
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        b_t = 87 if n == 1 else max(87 + bon[i] - (S - C[i]), 20)
        pw.append(c["f"] > b_t)
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not pw[i]: continue
        if n == 1: fills[i] = 87; continue
        N = c["f"] + c["rec7"] + c["nfunc"]
        t = float(N) + sum((5.0/6.0 if (cells[j]["sqa"] and (c["tup"]+c["uni"])>0) else 1.0) * C[j] for j in range(n) if j != i)
        fills[i] = max(20, min(hd(87.0*N/t), max(c["f"]-1, 20)))
    final = list(pw)
    if n > 1:
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if not pw[i]: continue
            tot = 0
            for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
                if j == i: continue
                truly = pw[j] and fills[j] is not None and fills[j] < cj["f"]-2
                tot += fills[j] if truly else C[j]
            if c["f"] <= max(87 - tot, 20):
                final[i] = False
    return pw, fills, final, C, bon

ff_ctx = collections.Counter(); fw_ctx = collections.Counter()
ff_margin = collections.Counter(); fw_margin = collections.Counter()
for fname, kind, cells in corpus:
    pwL, fillsL, finalL, C, bonL = model(cells, "last")
    pwS, fillsS, finalS, _, bonS = model(cells, "single")
    n = len(cells)
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        # FF under last: obs wrap, pred flat, bonus was load-bearing
        if c["wrap"] and not finalL[i] and bonL[i] > 0:
            sib_truly = any(pwL[j] and fillsL[j] is not None and fillsL[j] < cells[j]["f"]-2 for j in range(n) if j != i)
            sib_wrap_obs = any(cells[j]["wrap"] for j in range(n) if j != i)
            ff_ctx[(n, sib_wrap_obs, sib_truly)] += 1
            # margin: flat - bonus-free budget
            b0 = max(87 - (sum(C) - C[i]), 20)
            ff_margin[min(c["f"] - b0, 9)] += 1
        # extra FW under single: obs flat, single-pred wrap, last-pred flat
        if (not c["wrap"]) and finalS[i] and not finalL[i]:
            sib_truly = any(pwS[j] and fillsS[j] is not None and fillsS[j] < cells[j]["f"]-2 for j in range(n) if j != i)
            sib_wrap_obs = any(cells[j]["wrap"] for j in range(n) if j != i)
            fw_ctx[(n, sib_wrap_obs, sib_truly)] += 1
            b0 = max(87 - (sum(C) - C[i]), 20)
            fw_margin[min(c["f"] - b0, 9)] += 1
print("FF-under-last (ref WRAPS a bonus-holding cell): (n, sib_wrap_obs, sib_truly_pred):")
for k, v in ff_ctx.most_common(12): print("  ", k, v)
print("margin flat - bonusfree_budget:", dict(sorted(ff_margin.items())))
print("\nextra-FW-under-single (ref keeps flat, needs bonus): (n, sib_wrap_obs, sib_truly_pred):")

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-11 batteries G/H/I: multi-cell fill allocation + wrap relief.

G (probeG.spthy): 2-cell conclusion rows where BOTH cells wrap — coarse grid of
  (narrow, wide) flats + near-equal pairs + corpus witness controls.  Reads the
  per-cell allocated ribbons via band extraction (the round-10 under-sampled
  interaction: every prior fill battery had fitting/unbreakable siblings).
H (probeH.spthy): 3- and 4-cell rows mixing wrapping and flat cells — pins how
  a wrapping sibling is charged in the others' fills (post-wrap alloc vs C).
I (probeI.spthy): family-3 trigger relief — a target cell T beside a WRAPPING
  wide sibling W: sweep T's flat across the boundary to find where T stops
  fitting, as a function of W's flat; with tuple-carrying T variants and a
  3-cell variant.

All cells are plain argfacts of distinct $-vars (one longvar to hit an exact
flat + 3-char pvars for tight bands); no term repeats within a rule, so no
abbreviation fires (len>=10 needs occ>=2)."""

def pvars(n, off=0):
    a = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
    return ["$" + a[(i + off) // 26] + a[(i + off) % 26] for i in range(n)]

def longvar(ln, tag):
    body = ("q" + tag).ljust(ln - 1, "a")
    return "$" + body

def argfact(name, flat, tag, off):
    """`name( LONG, $aa, $ab, ... )` of exact display width `flat`."""
    minL = 2 + len(tag)  # "$q<tag>" cannot shrink below this
    k = (flat - len(name) - 4 - minL) // 5

        k = 0
    L1 = flat - len(name) - 4 - 5 * k
    assert L1 >= minL, (name, flat, k, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(k, off)
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    # rendered form pads exactly like this; sanity-check width
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

def pairfact(name, flat, tag, off, inner_n=2):
    """`name( <LONG, $aa[, ...]> )` — one top-level tuple arg (tup_sur =
    inner_n+1)."""
    fixed = len(name) + 2 + 1 + (inner_n - 1) * 5 + 1 + 2  # name( <...> )
    L1 = flat - fixed
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(inner_n - 1, off)
    s = f"{name}( <{', '.join(elems)}> )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}(<{', '.join(elems)}>)"

class B:
    def __init__(s, thy, builtins=None):
        s.thy, s.builtins = thy, builtins
        s.rules, s.lemmas, s.names, s.n = [], [], [], 0
    def emit(s, name, prems, concls, comment):
        s.rules.append(f"// {comment}\nrule {name}:\n  [ {', '.join(prems)} ]\n"
                       f"  --[ F{s.n}() ]->\n  [ {', '.join(concls)} ]\n")
        s.lemmas.append(f"lemma l_{name}:\n  exists-trace \"Ex #i. F{s.n}() @ #i\"\n")
        s.names.append(name)

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Round-12 batteries L/M/N/O.

L (probeL.spthy): pass-1 self-budget bonus for MULTI-ARG facts whose LAST arg
  is a tuple/union (family B).  Beside a floor-protected sib (flat 20, never
  wraps, C = 20; bonus-free budget 67), sweep the target flat across the
  boundary for tuple sizes n = 2,3,4,6, lead args 1..2, plus single-arg /
  mid-list / union controls.  Corpus hypothesis: multi-arg slack = fl(n/2)+1
  (one less than the single-arg fl(n/2)+2).
M (probeM.spthy): relief-charge law on PLAIN targets: [wrapping W, target T at
  the relief boundary].  Hybrid hypothesis: a wrapping sib charges its fill
  when deeply broken (fill < flat-4) else fill+1; W ~ 87+g puts the sib's
  break gap at g with T at the boundary.  Plus rec-sib cap rows (charge
  min(fill+1, C) vs C).
N (probeN.spthy): fill-numerator terms: nfunc exclusion (corpus: functions do
  NOT enter N), quoted-constant discount (corpus: nq-negative bias), rec cap
  at 7 for 7/8-elem receivers, and the fill-denominator charge for BIG-tuple
  sibs (10/16-elem: rec vs rec7).
O (probeO.spthy): trigger margins: all-plain rows with C-total 88/89 (which
  cells stay flat at budget+1), n=3 versions, corpus FW replicas (quote/pair
  rows), and the [41w, 51 deep-nested single-arg pair] FW witness replica.
"""
import math

def pvars(n, off=0):
    a = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
    return ["$" + a[(i + off) // 26] + a[(i + off) % 26] for i in range(n)]

def longvar(ln, tag):
    body = ("q" + tag).ljust(ln - 1, "a")
    assert len(body) + 1 == ln
    return "$" + body

def argfact(name, flat, tag, off):
    minL = 2 + len(tag)
    k = (flat - len(name) - 4 - minL) // 5
    if k < 0:
        k = 0
    L1 = flat - len(name) - 4 - 5 * k
    assert L1 >= minL, (name, flat, k, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(k, off)
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

def pairfact(name, flat, tag, off, inner_n=2):
    fixed = len(name) + 2 + 1 + (inner_n - 1) * 5 + 1 + 2
    L1 = flat - fixed
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(inner_n - 1, off)
    s = f"{name}( <{', '.join(elems)}> )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}(<{', '.join(elems)}>)"

def mtupfact(name, flat, tag, off, lead, inner_n):
    """`name( LONG, $aa[, ...], <$b1, ..., $bn> )` — lead plain args then a
    LAST tuple of inner_n 3-char vars."""
    tup = "<" + ", ".join(pvars(inner_n, off + 10)) + ">"
    fixed = len(name) + 4 + 5 * (lead - 1) + 2 + len(tup)
    L1 = flat - fixed
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + pvars(lead - 1, off) + [tup]
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

def midtupfact(name, flat, tag, off, inner_n):
    """`name( LONG, <...>, $aa )` — tuple mid-list (2nd of 3)."""
    tup = "<" + ", ".join(pvars(inner_n, off + 10)) + ">"
    fixed = len(name) + 4 + 2 + len(tup) + 5
    L1 = flat - fixed
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    s = f"{name}( {longvar(L1, tag)}, {tup}, {pvars(1, off)[0]} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({longvar(L1, tag)}, {tup}, {pvars(1, off)[0]})"

def muni fact():  # placeholder to fail loudly if called
    raise RuntimeError

def munifact(name, flat, tag, off, inner_n):
    """`name( LONG, (a++b++c) )` — last arg an inner_n-union of 3-char vars."""
    uni = "(" + "++".join(pvars(inner_n, off + 10)) + ")"
    fixed = len(name) + 4 + 2 + len(uni)
    L1 = flat - fixed
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    s = f"{name}( {longvar(L1, tag)}, {uni} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({longvar(L1, tag)}, {uni})"

def funcfact(name, flat, tag, off, nfunc):
    """`name( LONG, w1($aa), ... )` — nfunc unary applications."""
    funcs = [f"w1({v})" for v in pvars(nfunc, off)]
    fixed = len(name) + 4 + sum(len(f) + 2 for f in funcs) - 2 + 2
    L1 = flat - len(name) - 4 - sum(len(f) + 2 for f in funcs)
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + funcs
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

def quotefact(name, flat, tag, off, nq):
    """`name( LONG, 'ab', 'cd', ... )` — nq quoted constants."""
    qs = ["'q%d%s'" % (i, "abcdefgh"[i]) for i in range(nq)]
    L1 = flat - len(name) - 4 - sum(len(q) + 2 for q in qs)
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)
    elems = [longvar(L1, tag)] + qs
    s = f"{name}( {', '.join(elems)} )"
    assert len(s) == flat, (s, len(s), flat)
    return f"{name}({', '.join(elems)})"

def hd(x):
    fl = math.floor(x)
    return int(fl) if abs(x - fl - 0.5) < 1e-9 else int(math.floor(x + 0.5))

class B:
    def __init__(s, thy, extra=""):
        s.thy, s.extra = thy, extra
        s.rules, s.lemmas, s.names, s.n = [], [], [], 0
    def emit(s, name, prems, concls, comment):
        s.rules.append(f"// {comment}\nrule {name}:\n  [ {', '.join(prems)} ]\n"
                       f"  --[ F{s.n}() ]->\n  [ {', '.join(concls)} ]\n")
        s.lemmas.append(f"lemma l_{name}:\n  exists-trace \"Ex #i. F{s.n}() @ #i\"\n")
        s.names.append(name)
        s.n += 1
    def write(s, path):
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            f.write(f"theory {s.thy}\nbegin\n\n")
            if s.extra:
                f.write(s.extra + "\n")
            f.write("\n")
            for r in s.rules:
                f.write(r + "\n")
            for l in s.lemmas:
                f.write(l + "\n")
            f.write("end\n")
        with open(path.replace(".spthy", ".names"), "w") as f:
            f.write("\n".join(s.names) + "\n")
        print(f"wrote {path}: {len(s.rules)} rules")

prem = [f"In(<{', '.join(pvars(2, 100))}>)"]

# ---------------- L: multi-arg last-tuple bonus ----------------
L = B("BonusGateProbe", "builtins: multiset")
SIB = 20  # floor-protected sib, never wraps; bonus-free budget 67
for n, flats in ((2, (68, 69, 70, 71)), (3, (68, 69, 70, 71)),
                 (4, (69, 70, 71, 72, 73)), (6, (70, 71, 72, 73))):
    for fl in flats:
        L.emit(f"LA{n}_{fl}", prem,
               [argfact("Fzz", SIB, f"{L.n:02d}", 0),
                mtupfact("Mtt", fl, f"x{L.n:02d}", 30, 2, n)],
               f"LA: multi-arg(2 lead) last-{n}-tuple flat {fl} beside flat-20 (budget0 67)")
for n, flats in ((2, (69, 70, 71)), (4, (70, 71, 72))):
    for fl in flats:
        L.emit(f"LB{n}_{fl}", prem,
               [argfact("Fzz", SIB, f"{L.n:02d}", 0),
                mtupfact("Mtt", fl, f"x{L.n:02d}", 30, 1, n)],
               f"LB: multi-arg(1 lead) last-{n}-tuple flat {fl}")
for fl in (69, 70, 71):
    L.emit(f"LC3_{fl}", prem,
           [argfact("Fzz", SIB, f"{L.n:02d}", 0),
            pairfact("Stt", fl, f"x{L.n:02d}", 30, 3)],
           f"LC: single-arg 3-tuple control flat {fl} (expect fits <= 70)")
for fl in (67, 68, 69):
    L.emit(f"LD4_{fl}", prem,
           [argfact("Fzz", SIB, f"{L.n:02d}", 0),
            midtupfact("Dtt", fl, f"x{L.n:02d}", 30, 4)],
           f"LD: mid-list 4-tuple control flat {fl} (expect fits <= 67)")
for fl in (68, 69, 70):
    L.emit(f"LE3_{fl}", prem,
           [argfact("Fzz", SIB, f"{L.n:02d}", 0),
            munifact("Utt", fl, f"x{L.n:02d}", 30, 3)],
           f"LE: multi-arg last-3-union flat {fl}")
L.write("probeL.spthy")

# ---------------- M: relief-charge law, plain targets ----------------
M = B("ReliefChargeProbe")
def fill2(w, t, cw=None, ct=None):
    cw = cw if cw is not None else w
    ct = ct if ct is not None else t
    b = hd(87.0 * w / (w + ct))
    return max(20, min(b, max(w - 1, 20)))
rows = []
for W in (88, 89, 90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 96):
    T = 40
    for _ in range(30):
        f = fill2(W, T)
        T2 = 87 - f
        if T2 == T:
            break
        T = T2
    f = fill2(W, T)
    gap = W - f
    rows.append((W, T, f, gap))
for W, T, f, gap in rows:
    for dt in (-1, 0, 1):
        M.emit(f"MA_{W}_{T + dt}", prem,
               [argfact("Wbb", W, f"{M.n:02d}", 0), argfact("Tcc", T + dt, f"x{M.n:02d}", 30)],
               f"MA: relief boundary [W {W} fill~{f} gap {gap}, T {T + dt}] (boundary T {T})")
# MC: shallow-broken sib WITH occupancy surcharge (pair sib, rec 3):
# charge min(fill+1, C) vs C differ by 2 at gap 2.
for W, dt in ((60, -1), (60, 0), (60, 1), (62, 0)):
    # pair sib: C = W+3; target boundary under fill+1 charge
    T = 40
    for _ in range(30):
        f = fill2(W, T, cw=W + 3)
        T2 = 87 - (f + 1)
        if T2 == T:
            break
        T = T2
    f = fill2(W, T, cw=W + 3)
    M.emit(f"MC_{W}_{T + dt}", prem,
           [pairfact("Pbb", W, f"{M.n:02d}", 0, 2), argfact("Tcc", T + dt, f"x{M.n:02d}", 30)],
           f"MC: pair-sib relief [P {W} rec3 fill~{f}, T {T + dt}] (fill+1 boundary T {T})")
M.write("probeM.spthy")

# ---------------- N: fill numerator / denominator ----------------
N = B("FillTermsProbe", "functions: w1/1")
def pick_rows(mk_target, nlab, nvals, svals, numf, numf_alt, crange=3):
    """Emit rows where hd of the two numerator laws differ."""
    out = []
    for t in nvals:
        for s in svals:
            n1 = numf(t)
            n2 = numf_alt(t)
            b1 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * n1 / (n1 + s)), t - 1))
            b2 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * n2 / (n2 + s)), t - 1))
            if b1 != b2 and t > 87 - s and s > 87 - t:  # both wrap (plain sib)
                out.append((t, s, b1, b2))
    return out[:crange]
# NA: nfunc 2 and 4 — N=flat (f7) vs N=flat+nfunc (f7n)
for nf in (2, 4):
    cnt = 0
    for t in range(58, 78):
        for s in range(40, 72, 1):
            b_f7 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * t / (t + s)), t - 1))
            b_f7n = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * (t + nf) / (t + nf + s)), t - 1))
            if b_f7 != b_f7n and t > 87 - s and s > 87 - t and cnt < 4:
                N.emit(f"NA{nf}_{t}_{s}", prem,
                       [funcfact("Ftt", t, f"{N.n:02d}", 0, nf), argfact("Sbb", s, f"x{N.n:02d}", 30)],
                       f"NA: {nf}-func target {t} beside {s}: f7 fill {b_f7} vs f7n {b_f7n}")
                cnt += 1
# NB: nq 2 and 4 — N=flat vs flat-1q vs flat-2q
for nq in (2, 4):
    cnt = 0
    for t in range(58, 78):
        for s in range(40, 72):
            b0 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * t / (t + s)), t - 1))
            b1 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * (t - nq) / (t - nq + s)), t - 1))
            b2 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * (t - 2 * nq) / (t - 2 * nq + s)), t - 1))
            if len({b0, b1, b2}) == 3 and t > 87 - s and s > 87 - t and cnt < 4:
                N.emit(f"NB{nq}_{t}_{s}", prem,
                       [quotefact("Qtt", t, f"{N.n:02d}", 0, nq), argfact("Sbb", s, f"x{N.n:02d}", 30)],
                       f"NB: {nq}-quote target {t} beside {s}: fills q0 {b0} / q-1 {b1} / q-2 {b2}")
                cnt += 1
# NC: 7- and 8-elem tuple receivers — rec cap 7 vs elems+1
for ne in (7, 8):
    cnt = 0
    sur_cap, sur_full = 7, ne + 1
    for t in range(62, 82):
        for s in range(40, 72):
            bc = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * (t + sur_cap) / (t + sur_cap + s)), t - 1))
            bf = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * (t + sur_full) / (t + sur_full + s)), t - 1))
            if bc != bf and t > 87 - s + 4 and s > 87 - (t + sur_full) and cnt < 4:
                N.emit(f"NC{ne}_{t}_{s}", prem,
                       [pairfact("Rtt", t, f"{N.n:02d}", 0, ne), argfact("Sbb", s, f"x{N.n:02d}", 30)],
                       f"NC: {ne}-tuple receiver {t} beside {s}: cap7 fill {bc} vs full {bf}")
                cnt += 1
# ND: plain target beside BIG-tuple sib (10/16-elem): denominator C vs C7
for ne in (10, 16):
    cnt = 0
    rec, rec7 = ne + 1, 7
    for t in range(50, 80):
        for s in range(56 if ne == 10 else 86, 110):
            bC = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * t / (t + s + rec)), t - 1))
            bC7 = max(20, min(hd(87.0 * t / (t + s + rec7)), t - 1))
            if bC != bC7 and t > 87 - (s + rec) and s > 87 - (t + 0) and cnt < 4:
                N.emit(f"ND{ne}_{t}_{s}", prem,
                       [argfact("Ttt", t, f"{N.n:02d}", 0), pairfact("Gbb", s, f"x{N.n:02d}", 30, ne)],
                       f"ND: plain {t} beside {ne}-tuple sib {s}: D=C fill {bC} vs C7 {bC7}")
                cnt += 1
N.write("probeN.spthy")

# ---------------- O: trigger margins ----------------
O = B("TriggerMarginProbe", "functions: w1/1")
for a, b in ((45, 43), (46, 42), (44, 44), (47, 41), (50, 38), (55, 33),
             (60, 28), (65, 23), (45, 44), (46, 43), (50, 39)):
    O.emit(f"OA_{a}_{b}", prem,
           [argfact("Aaa", a, f"{O.n:02d}", 0), argfact("Bbb", b, f"x{O.n:02d}", 30)],
           f"OA: plain pair [{a}, {b}] total {a + b}")
for tri in ((20, 39, 29), (30, 30, 28), (40, 24, 24), (29, 30, 29)):
    a, b, c = tri
    O.emit(f"OB_{a}_{b}_{c}", prem,
           [argfact("Aaa", a, f"{O.n:02d}", 0), argfact("Bbb", b, f"x{O.n:02d}", 30),
            argfact("Ccc", c, f"y{O.n:02d}", 60)],
           f"OB: plain triple {tri} total {sum(tri)}")
# OC: corpus FW replica [pair34(rec4? use 3-tuple: rec 4), 22, pair25]: sweep mid
for mid in (21, 22, 23, 24):
    O.emit(f"OC_{mid}", prem,
           [pairfact("Paa", 34, f"{O.n:02d}", 0, 3), argfact("Tbb", mid, f"x{O.n:02d}", 30),
            pairfact("Qcc", 25, f"y{O.n:02d}", 60, 2)],
           f"OC: FW replica [34(3-tup rec4), {mid}, 25(pair rec3)] — ref keeps {mid} flat?")
# OD: [41w mid-pair+func, 51 deep-nested single-arg pair] replica; sweep target
def deeppair(name, flat, tag, off):
    """`name( <w1(<LONG, $b, $c>), <$d, $e>> )` — rec 3+4+1 = 8, nfunc 1."""
    inner = pvars(4, off)
    core = f"<w1(<LNG, {inner[0]}, {inner[1]}>), <{inner[2]}, {inner[3]}>>"
    L1 = flat - (len(name) + 4 + len(core) - 3)
    assert L1 >= 2 + len(tag), (name, flat, L1)

import math, glob, sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from eval12b import parse22, band_runs, in_band, hd

def run(rows, nlaw, rlaw):
    ff = fw = fill_hit = fill_tot = 0
    misses = []
    for fname, kind, cells in rows:
        n = len(cells)
        C = [c["f"] + c["rec"] + c["ftup"] for c in cells]
        S = sum(C)
        s1 = [min(c["smax"], 4) for c in cells]
        N = [c["f"] + c["rec7"] + (c["ftup"] if nlaw == "ft" else 0) for c in cells]
        pw, fills, qs = [], [None]*n, [None]*n
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            b_t = 87 if n == 1 else max(87 + s1[i] - (S - C[i]), 20)
            pw.append(c["f"] > b_t)
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if not pw[i]: continue
            if n == 1: fills[i] = 87; qs[i] = 87.0; continue
            t = float(N[i]) + sum((5.0/6.0 if (cells[j]["sqa"] and (c["tup"]+c["uni"])>0) else 1.0)*C[j] for j in range(n) if j != i)
            qs[i] = 87.0*N[i]/t
            fills[i] = max(20, min(hd(qs[i]), max(c["f"]-1, 20)))
        final = list(pw)
        if n > 1:
            for i, c in enumerate(cells):
                if not pw[i]: continue
                bump = 0.0 if (rlaw == "q13e4" and c["bonus"] >= 4) else 1.0/3.0
                tot = 0
                for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
                    if j == i: continue
                    if pw[j] and fills[j] is not None:
                        tot += min(hd(qs[j] + bump), C[j])
                    else:
                        tot += C[j]
                if c["f"] <= max(87 - tot, 20):
                    final[i] = False
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if final[i] and not c["wrap"]: fw += 1
            elif not final[i] and c["wrap"]: ff += 1
            elif c["wrap"]:
                fill_tot += 1
                if fills[i] is not None and in_band(c["st"], 3*fills[i]//2): fill_hit += 1
    return ff, fw, fill_hit, fill_tot

corpus = parse22("bands12.tsv")
probes = []
for p in sorted(glob.glob("b12_p*.tsv")):
    probes.extend(parse22(p))
print(f"{'config':26s} {'corpFF':>6} {'FW':>5} {'fill':>15} | {'prbFF':>5} {'FW':>4} {'fill':>11}")
for nlaw in ("ft", "f7"):
    for rlaw in ("q13", "q13e4"):
        cf = run(corpus, nlaw, rlaw)
        pf = run(probes, nlaw, rlaw)
        print(f"N={nlaw} R={rlaw:6s}            {cf[0]:>6} {cf[1]:>5} {cf[2]:>7}/{cf[3]:<7} | {pf[0]:>5} {pf[1]:>4} {pf[2]:>5}/{pf[3]:<5}")

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '.')
from eval12b import parse22, band_runs, in_band, hd
rows = parse22("b12_pK.tsv")
def run(cells):
    n = len(cells)
    C = [c["f"] + c["rec"] + c["ftup"] for c in cells]
    S = sum(C)
    s1 = [min(c["smax"], 4) for c in cells]
    N = [c["f"] + c["rec7"] + c["ftup"] for c in cells]
    pw, fills, qs = [], [None]*n, [None]*n
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        b_t = 87 if n == 1 else max(87 + s1[i] - (S - C[i]), 20)
        pw.append(c["f"] > b_t)
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        if not pw[i]: continue
        if n == 1: fills[i] = 87; qs[i] = 87.0; continue
        t = float(N[i]) + sum((5.0/6.0 if (cells[j]["sqa"] and (c["tup"]+c["uni"])>0) else 1.0)*C[j] for j in range(n) if j != i)
        qs[i] = 87.0*N[i]/t
        fills[i] = max(20, min(hd(qs[i]), max(c["f"]-1, 20)))
    final = list(pw)
    if n > 1:
        for i, c in enumerate(cells):
            if not pw[i]: continue
            bump = 0.0 if c["bonus"] >= 4 else 1.0/3.0
            tot = 0
            for j, cj in enumerate(cells):
                if j == i: continue
                tot += min(hd(qs[j]+bump), C[j]) if (pw[j] and fills[j] is not None) else C[j]
            if c["f"] <= max(87 - tot, 20):
                final[i] = False
    return pw, fills, final
for fname, kind, cells in rows:
    pw, fills, final = run(cells)
    for i, c in enumerate(cells):
        ok = (final[i] == c["wrap"]) and (not c["wrap"] or c["st"]=="NONE" or (fills[i] is not None and in_band(c["st"], 3*fills[i]//2)))
        if not ok:
            print(fname, "cell", i, "flat", c["f"], "obs", c["st"][:14], "predw", final[i], "fill", fills[i], [(cj["f"],cj["rec"],cj["ftup"],cj["wrap"]) for cj in cells])
EOF

# graph-clean — adapter-facing width-input interface (round 12)

Audience: the OPEN-side integrator wiring an adapter later. Everything here is
implemented and test-gated in `workspace/graph-clean`; semantics trace to
BEHAVIOR.md §3f (Sessions 10–12).

## Round-11 status (unchanged): internal widths are NOT how the reference decides

Round-11 live probes (battery J, QUERIES.log Session 11) REFUTED the round-9/10
belief that the reference wraps cells on their UN-abbreviated internal widths:
abbreviated cells with internal widths 96–150 render FLAT when their display
text fits, and a sibling's budget follows the abbreviated display occupancy.
The reference lays out the POST-abbreviation display text everywhere probed.
Consequently an adapter should normally pass **no overrides at all** — the
display-text estimates are the probed reference behavior. The override surface
remains for callers that know better in exceptional cases, and every field
falls back per-field to the estimate, byte-identically.

## Surface

```rust
pub struct generate::CellWidths {
    pub occupancy:     Option<i64>, // row occupancy C (columns this cell counts
                                    // for in siblings' budgets + fill shares)
    pub bonus:         Option<i64>, // the cell's own pass-1 trigger SLACK
    pub fill_width:    Option<i64>, // fill numerator once the cell wraps
    pub trigger_width: Option<i64>, // effective self width in BOTH trigger
                                    // passes (incl. lone cells vs 87)
}
pub fn generate::group_widths_with(cells: &[String],
                                   overrides: &[Option<CellWidths>]) -> Vec<usize>;
// RawRule builders (one Option<CellWidths> per premise / conclusion cell):
RawRule::premise_widths(Vec<Option<CellWidths>>)
RawRule::conclusion_widths(Vec<Option<CellWidths>>)
```

## Semantics (precise, round-12 laws)

Per record group (premises, or conclusions; the info cell is its own group):

- **occupancy**: `C_i = flat_i + rec_sur_i + ftup_i` where `rec_sur` is the
  recursive tuple/union surcharge — each tuple/union node contributes
  `elems + 1`, except nodes directly inside another tuple/union which
  contribute `elems − 1` — and `ftup` counts function-application nodes
  INSIDE a tuple/union subtree (top-level funcs are uncharged);
- **trigger pass 1**: cell *i* wraps iff
  `eff_i > max(87 + slack_i − Σ_{j≠i} C_j, 20)` (lone cell: budget exactly
  87), `eff_i` = display flat (or `trigger_width`), `slack_i` = the largest
  `⌈elems/2⌉ − 1` over top-level tuple/union args in ANY position (mid-list
  included), capped at 4 — round-12 battery L; the round-10/11 last-gated
  `⌊elems/2⌋ + 2` bonus is refuted;
- **fill** (only if wrapping): ribbon
  `clamp(hd(87·N_i / (N_i + Σ_{j≠i} w_j·C_j)), 20, flat_i − 1)` rounded
  half-DOWN, `N_i = flat_i + rec_sur7_i + ftup_i` (per-node tuple surcharges
  capped at 7; NO top-level-func term, NO quote discount), `w_j = 5/6` for a
  single-quoted-atom sibling when cell *i* has a top-level tuple/union arg,
  else 1; the cell is laid out at lineLength `⌊3·ribbon/2⌋`, ribbonsPerLine
  1.5 (ragged HughesPJ fill; the tuple opener `<` may hang);
- **trigger pass 2 (relief)**: a pass-1-wrapping cell renders FLAT iff
  `eff_i ≤ max(87 − Σ_{j≠i} charge_j, 20)` where a pass-1-wrapping sibling
  charges `min(hd(q_j + bump_i), C_j)` on its UNROUNDED fill quotient `q_j`
  — `bump_i = 1/3`, dropped (0) when cell *i* carries a top-level
  tuple/union arg of ≥ 4 elements — and every flat sibling charges `C_j`;
  no slack in this comparison.

Overrides substitute per FIELD (`C_i` ← `occupancy`, `slack_i` ← `bonus`,
`N_i` ← `fill_width`, `eff_i` ← `trigger_width`); each absent field (or absent
entry, or a short/empty override slice) falls back to the display-text
estimate for that field. With every input absent the output is byte-identical
to `group_widths` — regression-gated by
`tests/generate_tests.rs::supplied_cell_widths_override_estimates`,
`::supplied_trigger_width_overrides_self_width`,
`::raw_rule_supplied_widths_reach_cells`, plus the corpus census.

## Known terminal residue (do not chase in an adapter)

Rows whose occupancies sum to exactly 88 (budget margin ±1..2) are decided by
the reference's coupled per-row `fits`; round-12 battery O proves no function
of the cell widths reproduces them all ([45,43] keeps the 43 flat, [46,42]
wraps the 42, etc.). The shipped model takes the best discrete tie-break;
~1,477 corpus cells (0.24 %) sit in this zone.
